titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Platt Lynes Diaries and Memorabilia
titlestmt.author: by Timothy G. Young

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4664691.
This collection contains 17 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 1,077 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 6, 2025 at 23:50

did.unittitle: George Platt Lynes diaries and memorabilia

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The George Platt Lynes diaries consist of diaries and datebooks which record his activities and thoughts, starting with a journal of Lynes' first trip to Europe in 1925 and ending with entries in a pocket datebook just weeks before his death in 1955. Though his early and later years are well documented, there are no diaries for the years 1931-1941. Accompanying the diaries are a few odd pieces of personal memorabilia, including a notebook of dinners given during the early 1940s and printed versions of writings by Glenway Wescott form the early 1920s.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Received with the Glenway Wescott papers, purchase from Monroe Wheeler, 1988.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The George Platt Lynes Diaries & Memorabilia are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Platt Lynes Diaries and Memorabilia. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional George Platt Lynes material can be found in the Glenway Wescott Papers [YCAL MSS 134] and the Monroe Wheeler Papers [YCAL MSS 136]. The materials included in this collection represent items that were already segregated when received with the original papers.

bioghist.head: GEORGE PLATT LYNES [1907-1955]
bioghist.p: George Platt Lynes is best known as a portrait, fashion, and nude photographer. He spent several years living with and traveling with Glenway Wescott and Monroe Wheeler.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
The George Platt Lynes Diaries & Memorabilia is organized into two series. Series I, Diaries
, the largest component, consists of diaries and datebooks which record his activities and thoughts, starting with a journal of Lynes' first trip to Europe in 1925 and ending with entries in a pocket datebook just weeks before his death in 1955. Though his early and later years are well documented, there are no diaries for the years 1931-1941. Series II, Memorabilia
, consists of a few odd personal items, including a notebook of dinners given during the early 1940s and printed versions of writings by Glenway Wescott.

did.unittitle: Drawing of GPL by Jean Cocteau [photocopy]

descriptivenote.p: Drawing of GPL by Jean Cocteau [photocopy]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes guest lists and draft of letter [by Monroe Wheeler?]

did.unittitle: Printed versions of writings by Glenway Wescott, collected by GPL
Receipt for tires and product information flyer
